text: This study outlines a community service initiative at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan designed to foster economic independence and entrepreneurial character among students using the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) framework. By focusing on 10th and 11th-grade students, the program integrated theoretical instruction on Islamic business ethics (mu’amalah) with practical experience within the pesantren’s existing business ecosystem, including ventures in culinary arts, bottled water production, mushroom cultivation, and aquaculture. Results indicate a significant improvement in student engagement and understanding, with many participants demonstrating a new ability to navigate the practical challenges of business management. By leveraging local assets and religious foundations, the program successfully empowered students with sustainable professional skills while reinforcing the social and spiritual values essential to a sharia-compliant economy. Ultimately, this initiative transformed the pesantren’s business units into vital learning laboratories, providing a replicable model for developing self-reliant, character-driven entrepreneurs in a religious educational setting.
